title: Stone and Dewdrop and the Stuck Fish

pageTimings.text:
Dewdrop was at the Crystal Stream.
She was looking at something in the water.
It was a large fish.
It was stuck.

The fish was wedged between two rocks.
It had been there since morning.
Its tail kept flicking.
It could not get free.

Dewdrop tried to push it loose.
She sent the water flowing hard against the rocks.
The fish did not move.
"I need help," said Dewdrop.

Dewdrop found Stone at the Hollow Oak.
"I need your hands," she said.
"Just to reach in and move a rock."
Stone understood immediately.
Then Stone understood all of it.

Stone stood at the edge of the Crystal Stream.
The water sparkled.
Stone's feet stayed on the bank.
"I don't like getting wet," said Stone.

"Just your arm," said Dewdrop.
"Just the one arm."
"Just for a moment."
Stone looked at the arm.
Stone looked at the water.
Stone sat down on the bank.

Stone rolled up one sleeve.
The arm was very large.
Stone leaned forward.
The hand went toward the water.

Stone's hand went into the stream.
It found the rock.
It gripped it.
Stone's face: very unhappy about this.

Stone pushed.
The rock moved.
The mud let go.
The fish was free.

The fish made a very large splash.
Stone was completely wet.
All of Stone.
Dewdrop was not wet.
"I'm sorry," said Dewdrop.

Stone sat and dripped.
Stone looked at the arm.
Stone looked at the rest of Stone.
"The fish is free," said Stone.
"Yes," said Dewdrop.

They sat by the stream together.
Stone dripped for a long time.
The fish surfaced once.
It seemed happy.
"Good," said Stone.
And meant it.
